detaileddescription.para: This page provides detailed documentation for the various graph classes used in the exp_mc20 analysis. These classes inherit from graph_template and define the specific nodes and features used to represent the event as a graph.

sect1.title: Graph of Reconstructed Jets
sect1.para: The graph_jets class constructs a graph using reconstructed jets and leptonic top decay products.

listitem.para: graph_jets(): Initializes the graph name to "graph_jets".
graph_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the graph_jets graph.

void CompileEvent(): Defines the graph structure and features.
Nodes: The nodes are the reconstructed jets and the leptonic/neutrino decay products of the top quarks.
Graph Truth Features: signal, n_lep, ntops.
Node Truth Features: top_node, res_node.
Edge Truth Features: res_edge, top_edge.
Graph Data Features: met, phi, num_jets, num_leps, weight, event_number.
Node Data Features: pt, eta, phi, energy, is_lep, is_b.

sect1.title: Graph of Reconstructed Jets (No Neutrinos)
sect1.para: The graph_jets_nonu class is similar to graph_jets, but excludes neutrinos from the graph nodes.

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: The nodes are the reconstructed jets and the leptonic (non-neutrino) decay products of the top quarks.
Neutrino Information: The double_neutrino() method is called to add information about the neutrinos.

sect1.title: Graph of Detector Objects (with Neutrinos)
sect1.para: The graph_jets_detector_lep class constructs a graph using all detector-level objects (jets, electrons, muons) and truth neutrinos.

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: Reconstructed jets, electrons, muons, and truth neutrinos.

sect1.title: Graph of Detector Objects
sect1.para: The graph_detector class constructs a graph using all detector-level objects (jets, electrons, muons).

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: Reconstructed jets, electrons, and muons.
Neutrino Information: The double_neutrino() method is called to add information about the neutrinos.
